content_en:
The first among the degrees of his happiness is to have a keen ear and heart that understands what the ear would hear, if he hears, realizes, sees the good path, sees that the majority of people are going astray, keeps to it and does not go astray. The reason behind going astray is refusing the oath, accepting it unwillingly, accept it while lacking power and determination, or failing to urge themselves to understand it, think about it, work according to it, or fulfill it. The oath was offered to them while still in their youth and observing religion as a habit and according to what they used to see their parents doing. They received the oath while feeling satisfied with what they saw from their parents and ancestors. This is not enough for whoever aims his intention and heart at understanding the oath and working according to it as if this oath applied to him alone and it was said to him: think about what this oath contains and work according to it. If he did not receive his oath in this way, he would follow his ancestors, the habits of his family, friends, neighbors, and the people of his country. He would be satisfied to take religion as a habit. If Satan seduces him and reaches his intention and determination, it would accuse him of clanship and zealotry to his fathers and ancestors and convince him that this is right and whatever disagrees with it is false; guidance would be considered evil and evil would be considered guidance. This would indeed be fanaticism, which is not based on knowledge. As he is satisfied to follow his family and nation, he will have what they have and will also have to pay what they are paying. He is far removed from guidance and Allah abandons him to whoever he took as a friend. He will see any guidance that disagrees with his nation and family as evil and false. If he has a higher intention, a more honorable soul, and a

higher worth, he would proceed to fulfill his oath and —

understand it and would know that the owner of this oath has a -

great stature that other people do not have. Through this oath -

he would know Allah. Allah will know that he knows Him, His ~

attributes, names, doings, and judgments. The servant will

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 279

know that Allah is self-existing, enables others to exist, is in no need of anyone while all others are in need of Him. That He established Himself on the Throne above all His creatures, that He sees, hears, accepts, becomes angry, loves, hates, manages the affairs of His kingdom, orders, and forbids, sends His Messengers to every place in His kingdom bearing His words, which are heard by whoever wishes from among His creatures. He stands firm on justice, awards according to both good and bad, is the Most Forbearing, the Most Merciful, the All-Thankful, the Most Generous, and the Well-doer. He is described with all perfection and is far above all defects, and He has no equal. The servant can see the wisdom of Allah in managing His kingdom and how He foreordains matters according to His will without opposing justice and wisdom. And when there is a confrontation between the mind, law, and nature, each one of them proved the truth in and of itself. The servant understands what Allah has described Himself with in His Book from the reality of His names that were revealed in His Book. The names with which Allah was introduced to His servants remain until the minds acknowledge Him and indeed nature testifies to Him.

If the servant knew and believed in his heart in the attributes of the owner of the oath, the light of these attributes would rise within his heart and would be as if he saw them with his own eyes. He would see their connection with creation and the commands of Allah, their effects in the sensual and spiritual world, and their effect on creatures; how they prevail, favor, come near, banish, give, and prevent. He would see with his heart the examples of His justice, favor, and mercy. Allah deserves to be worshiped according to irrefutable proof, valid judgments, perfect power, complete justice and wisdom, superiority above all His creatures as well as the fact that He is thorough, simultaneous, great, lofty, glorious, violent, and revengeful and with His mercy, He is charitable, kind, generous, forgiving, and patient. The servant would see the

280 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

irrefutable proof with valid judgments that any creature could not rebel against, and how these attributes agree with and are in harmony and prove each other. He would discover that wisdom prevails, which is the aim and end of all judgments, which are the beginning of all things, and how their divisions come back to the origins and principles of aims as if he is watching the principles of wisdom in action. He would notice how cases are based according to wisdom, justice, benefit, mercy, and charity. There is no case that deviates from that until the end of the world and all that exists and people are judged on the Day of Resurrection, which will show His justice, and wisdom, and the truth of His Messengers and what they said about Allah to mankind; including humans and jinn both believers and disbelievers. And then it would be revealed to His creatures from His attributes that which they did not know before that. Even those who knew Him best during this life would praise Him because of the perfection of His attributes and honored descriptions, which he did not know during this life. This would be shown to His creatures and the reasons would be also shown to them according to which people deviated, went astray, and became negligent. The difference between knowing the reality of His names and attributes and knowing them during this worldly life will be like the difference between Paradise and Hellfire and seeing them, and even greater than that.

It would be also understood from the oath how His names and attributes require the existence of prophethood, decrees, and not to leave His creatures live in vain, as well as requiring what is included in the forms of commands and prohibitions, and what is required in the existence of reward, punishment, and resurrection. This is necessary because of His s names and attributes as to be deemed far above all that His enemies claim. The servant would see in the oath the generality of power and its effect on all creatures, which did not even miss

AL-FAWA'D : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 281

one whit and that if Allah has any other gods alongside Him, the world would be destroyed, including the heavens, the earth, and all that is between them. He will see how Islam and the faith, which all creatures have comes from these Divine attributes, and how they require reward and punishment sooner or later. He will understand that it is impossible to combine acceptance of this oath and denying His attributes, His superiority above His creatures, communicating with man through His Books, and oaths. It also includes those who refuse to take this oath and fulfill it and even whoever accepted it but did not accept it as a whole.

Chapter: The Body and Soul

The body of the son of Adam was created trom the earth and his soul from the government of Heaven and then they were joined. If he is hungry, stays awake, and keeps his body busy in serving Allah, his soul will find itself lighter and more peaceful so that it would long for the place from which it was created and miss its heavenly world. But if he secures food, blessings, sleep, and rest, the body will incline to remain at the place from which it was created and the soul would be pulled along with it and be in a prison. If it was not for the fact that it would get used to that prison, it would ask for help, as a tortured person does, to find relief from the pain resulting from the separation and departure from its own world from which it was created.

In general, the more the body is light, the more the soul will be light and seek its heavenly world, and the more the body is heavy and seeks desires and relief, the heavier the soul will be and it will come down from its heavenly world and become lower and more earthly. You may see the soul of a man in Heaven while his body is on the earth. Another person serves Allah with his body and his soul is low and wandering about in lowly matters. When the soul separates from the body, it would either catch up with its high or low place. With the high, it would

find every blessing, happiness, beauty, pleasure, and good living while with the low it would find every distress, grief, suffering, sadness, and bad and difficult thing. Allah says,

which means, "But whosoever turns away from My Reminder (i.e. neither believes in this Qur'an nor acts on its orders, etc.) verily, for him is a life of hardship." (Taha, 20:124)

"My Reminder" is His words, which He revealed to His Messenger, and "turns away" is by neglecting and abandoning working according to these words, and the majority of explanations saw that "life of hardship" is torture in the grave. Ibn Mas'tid, Abu Hurairah, Abu Sa'id Al-Khudry, and Ibn ‘Abbas agreed on that andit has a traceable hadith.

This life of hardship is in return for relieving the body and soul with pleasures, desires and relief. The more you enrich the soul, it will restrain the heart until you live a life of hardship, and the more you restrain the soul, it will enrich the heart until that heart will be delighted and relieved. Hardship in this worldly life, following piety will find its relief in partition and the Hereafter, and relief in this worldly life following desires will find its hardship in the partition and the Hereafter. You should choose one of the two: the better, more pleasant, and more lasting. You may distress the body in order to relieve the soul but do not distress the soul to relieve the body; relief and distress of the soul is greater and more lasting while relief and distress of the body is shorter and easier.

A wise man will never order people to abandon life; as they cannot leave it, but he may order them to leave sins. Leaving life is a favor for them while leaving sins is an obligatory act of worship. How can they be ordered to do a favor while they do
content_id:
Yang pertama di antara tingkatan kebahagiaannya adalah memiliki pendengaran yang tajam dan hati yang memahami apa yang didengar telinga, jika ia mendengar, menyadari, melihat jalan kebaikan, melihat bahwa mayoritas manusia tersesat, berpegang teguh padanya dan tidak tersesat. Penyebab kesesatan adalah menolak perjanjian, menerimanya dengan enggan, menerimanya tanpa kekuatan dan tekad, atau gagal mendorong diri mereka untuk memahaminya, merenungkannya, mengamalkannya, atau memenuhinya. Perjanjian itu ditawarkan kepada mereka saat masih muda dan menjalankan agama sebagai kebiasaan dan sesuai dengan apa yang biasa mereka lihat dari orang tua mereka. Mereka menerima perjanjian itu sambil merasa puas dengan apa yang mereka lihat dari orang tua dan nenek moyang mereka. Ini tidaklah cukup bagi siapa pun yang mengarahkan niat dan hatinya untuk memahami perjanjian dan mengamalkannya seolah-olah perjanjian ini hanya ditujukan kepadanya saja dan dikatakan kepadanya: renungkanlah apa yang terkandung dalam perjanjian ini dan amalkanlah. Jika ia tidak menerima perjanjiannya dengan cara ini, ia akan mengikuti nenek moyangnya, kebiasaan keluarganya, teman-temannya, tetangganya, dan penduduk negerinya. Ia akan puas menjadikan agama sebagai kebiasaan semata. Jika setan menggodanya dan mencapai niat serta tekadnya, setan akan menuduhnya fanatik kesukuan dan ta'ashub kepada bapak-bapak dan nenek moyangnya serta meyakinkannya bahwa inilah yang benar dan apa pun yang bertentangan dengannya adalah batil; petunjuk akan dianggap kejahatan dan kejahatan akan dianggap petunjuk. Inilah sesungguhnya fanatisme yang tidak berdasarkan ilmu. Karena ia puas mengikuti keluarga dan bangsanya, ia akan mendapatkan apa yang mereka dapatkan dan juga harus membayar apa yang mereka bayar. Ia jauh dari petunjuk dan Allah menyerahkannya kepada siapa pun yang ia jadikan penolong. Ia akan melihat setiap petunjuk yang bertentangan dengan bangsa dan keluarganya sebagai kejahatan dan kebatilan. Jika ia memiliki niat yang lebih tinggi, jiwa yang lebih mulia, dan

nilai yang lebih tinggi, ia akan melanjutkan untuk memenuhi perjanjiannya dan —

memahaminya dan akan mengetahui bahwa pemilik perjanjian ini memiliki -

kedudukan agung yang tidak dimiliki orang lain. Melalui perjanjian ini -

ia akan mengenal Allah. Allah akan mengetahui bahwa ia mengenal-Nya, sifat-sifat-Nya, ~

nama-nama-Nya, perbuatan-perbuatan-Nya, dan hukum-hukum-Nya. Hamba tersebut akan

AL-FAWA'ID : Kumpulan Nasihat Bijak * 279

mengetahui bahwa Allah Maha Berdiri Sendiri, yang memungkinkan yang lain untuk ada, tidak membutuhkan siapa pun sementara semua yang lain membutuhkan-Nya. Bahwa Dia bersemayam di atas 'Arsy di atas semua makhluk-Nya, bahwa Dia melihat, mendengar, menerima, murka, mencintai, membenci, mengatur urusan kerajaan-Nya, memerintah, dan melarang, mengutus para Rasul-Nya ke setiap tempat di kerajaan-Nya membawa firman-Nya, yang didengar oleh siapa saja yang dikehendaki dari makhluk-Nya. Dia tegak di atas keadilan, memberi balasan menurut kebaikan dan keburukan, Maha Penyantun, Maha Penyayang, Maha Bersyukur, Maha Pemurah, dan Maha Berbuat Baik. Dia disifati dengan segala kesempurnaan dan Mahasuci dari segala kekurangan, dan Dia tidak ada tandingan-Nya. Hamba dapat melihat hikmah Allah dalam mengatur kerajaan-Nya dan bagaimana Dia menakdirkan segala urusan sesuai kehendak-Nya tanpa bertentangan dengan keadilan dan hikmah. Dan ketika terjadi pertentangan antara akal, syariat, dan fitrah, masing-masing membuktikan kebenaran pada dirinya sendiri. Hamba memahami apa yang Allah sifatkan tentang Diri-Nya dalam Kitab-Nya dari hakikat nama-nama-Nya yang diturunkan dalam Kitab-Nya. Nama-nama yang dengannya Allah memperkenalkan Diri-Nya kepada hamba-hamba-Nya tetap ada hingga akal mengakui-Nya dan sesungguhnya fitrah bersaksi tentang-Nya.

Jika hamba mengenal dan meyakini dalam hatinya sifat-sifat pemilik perjanjian tersebut, cahaya sifat-sifat itu akan terbit dalam hatinya dan akan seolah-olah ia melihatnya dengan mata kepalanya sendiri. Ia akan melihat keterkaitannya dengan ciptaan dan perintah-perintah Allah, pengaruhnya di alam inderawi dan rohani, dan pengaruhnya terhadap makhluk; bagaimana mereka menguasai, memberi karunia, mendekat, mengusir, memberi, dan mencegah. Ia akan melihat dengan hatinya contoh-contoh keadilan, karunia, dan rahmat-Nya. Allah berhak disembah berdasarkan bukti yang tak terbantahkan, hukum yang sah, kekuasaan yang sempurna, keadilan dan hikmah yang paripurna, keagungan di atas semua makhluk-Nya serta kenyataan bahwa Dia Maha Menyeluruh, serempak, Maha Agung, Maha Luhur, Maha Mulia, Maha Keras, dan Maha Membalas, dan dengan rahmat-Nya, Dia Maha Dermawan, Maha Lembut, Maha Pemurah, Maha Pengampun, dan Maha Penyabar. Hamba akan melihat

280 * AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Nasihat Bijak

bukti yang tak terbantahkan dengan hukum-hukum yang sah di mana tidak ada makhluk yang mampu memberontak terhadapnya, dan bagaimana sifat-sifat ini saling sesuai, selaras, dan saling membuktikan satu sama lain. Ia akan menemukan bahwa hikmah berlaku, yang merupakan tujuan dan akhir dari semua hukum, yang merupakan awal dari segala sesuatu, dan bagaimana cabang-cabangnya kembali kepada asal-usul dan prinsip-prinsip tujuan seolah-olah ia sedang menyaksikan prinsip-prinsip hikmah bekerja. Ia akan memperhatikan bagaimana perkara-perkara didasarkan menurut hikmah, keadilan, kemaslahatan, rahmat, dan kebaikan. Tidak ada perkara yang menyimpang dari itu hingga akhir dunia dan segala yang ada, dan manusia dihakimi pada Hari Kiamat, yang akan menunjukkan keadilan-Nya, dan hikmah-Nya, dan kebenaran para Rasul-Nya serta apa yang mereka sampaikan tentang Allah kepada umat manusia; termasuk manusia dan jin, baik yang beriman maupun yang kafir. Dan kemudian akan diungkapkan kepada makhluk-Nya dari sifat-sifat-Nya apa yang tidak mereka ketahui sebelumnya. Bahkan mereka yang paling mengenal-Nya selama kehidupan ini akan memuji-Nya karena kesempurnaan sifat-sifat-Nya dan deskripsi-deskripsi mulia-Nya, yang tidak ia ketahui selama kehidupan dunia ini. Hal ini akan ditunjukkan kepada makhluk-Nya dan sebab-sebab akan ditunjukkan pula kepada mereka mengenai mengapa manusia menyimpang, tersesat, dan menjadi lalai. Perbedaan antara mengetahui hakikat nama-nama dan sifat-sifat-Nya dan mengetahuinya selama kehidupan dunia ini akan bagaikan perbedaan antara Surga dan Neraka serta melihat keduanya, bahkan lebih besar dari itu.

Akan dipahami pula dari perjanjian tersebut bagaimana nama-nama dan sifat-sifat-Nya menuntut adanya kenabian, ketetapan-ketetapan, dan tidak membiarkan makhluk-Nya hidup sia-sia, serta menuntut apa yang tercakup dalam bentuk perintah dan larangan, dan apa yang dituntut dalam adanya pahala, hukuman, dan kebangkitan. Ini adalah keniscayaan karena nama-nama dan sifat-sifat-Nya agar Dia disucikan dari apa yang diklaim oleh musuh-musuh-Nya. Hamba akan melihat dalam perjanjian tersebut keumuman kekuasaan dan pengaruhnya terhadap semua makhluk, yang tidak melewatkan

AL-FAWA'ID : Kumpulan Nasihat Bijak * 281

sedikit pun, dan bahwa jika Allah memiliki tuhan-tuhan lain bersama-Nya, dunia akan hancur, termasuk langit, bumi, dan semua yang ada di antara keduanya. Ia akan melihat bagaimana Islam dan iman, yang dimiliki semua makhluk, berasal dari sifat-sifat Ilahiah ini, dan bagaimana sifat-sifat itu menuntut adanya pahala dan hukuman cepat atau lambat. Ia akan memahami bahwa mustahil menggabungkan penerimaan perjanjian ini dengan pengingkaran terhadap sifat-sifat-Nya, keagungan-Nya di atas makhluk-Nya, berkomunikasi dengan manusia melalui Kitab-Kitab-Nya, dan perjanjian-perjanjian-Nya. Termasuk juga mereka yang menolak untuk mengambil perjanjian ini dan memenuhinya dan bahkan siapa pun yang menerimanya tetapi tidak menerimanya secara keseluruhan.

Bab: Tubuh dan Jiwa

Tubuh anak Adam diciptakan dari tanah dan jiwanya dari pemerintahan langit, kemudian keduanya disatukan. Jika ia lapar, terjaga, dan menyibukkan tubuhnya dalam beribadah kepada Allah, jiwanya akan menemukan dirinya lebih ringan dan lebih tentram sehingga ia merindukan tempat dari mana ia diciptakan dan mengingat dunia langitnya. Namun jika ia mendapatkan makanan, kenikmatan, tidur, dan istirahat, tubuh akan cenderung untuk tetap di tempat dari mana ia diciptakan dan jiwa akan tertarik bersamanya dan berada dalam penjara. Seandainya bukan karena ia terbiasa dengan penjara itu, ia akan meminta pertolongan, sebagaimana orang yang disiksa, untuk menemukan kelegaan dari rasa sakit akibat perpisahan dan kepergian dari dunianya sendiri dari mana ia diciptakan.

Secara umum, semakin ringan tubuh, semakin ringan jiwa dan ia akan mencari dunia langitnya, dan semakin berat tubuh dan mencari keinginan serta kenyamanan, semakin berat jiwa dan ia akan turun dari dunia langitnya dan menjadi lebih rendah dan lebih duniawi. Engkau mungkin melihat jiwa seseorang di langit sementara tubuhnya di bumi. Orang lain beribadah kepada Allah dengan tubuhnya sementara jiwanya rendah dan berkeliaran dalam urusan-urusan yang hina. Ketika jiwa berpisah dari tubuh, ia akan menyusul ke tempat tinggi atau rendahnya. Dengan yang tinggi, ia akan

menemukan setiap keberkahan, kebahagiaan, keindahan, kenikmatan, dan kehidupan yang baik, sedangkan bersama yang rendah ia akan menemukan setiap kesusahan, kesedihan, penderitaan, duka, serta hal-hal yang buruk dan sulit. Allah berfirman,

yang artinya, "Dan barangsiapa berpaling dari peringatan-Ku (yakni tidak beriman kepada Al-Quran ini dan tidak mengamalkan perintah-perintahnya), maka sesungguhnya baginya penghidupan yang sempit." (Taha, 20:124)

"Peringatan-Ku" adalah firman-firman-Nya yang diturunkan kepada Rasul-Nya, dan "berpaling" adalah dengan mengabaikan dan meninggalkan pengamalan firman-firman tersebut, dan mayoritas penafsir berpendapat bahwa "penghidupan yang sempit" adalah azab kubur. Ibn Mas'ud, Abu Hurairah, Abu Sa'id Al-Khudry, dan Ibn 'Abbas sepakat dalam hal itu, dan terdapat hadith yang marfu' mengenainya.

Penghidupan yang sempit ini adalah balasan atas dimanjanya tubuh dan jiwa dengan kesenangan, syahwat, dan kenyamanan. Semakin engkau memperkaya jiwa (dengan kesenangan duniawi), semakin ia akan menyempitkan hati hingga engkau hidup dalam kesempitan, dan semakin engkau menahan jiwa, semakin ia akan memperkaya hati hingga hati itu akan bergembira dan tenteram. Kesusahan di dunia ini yang diikuti dengan taqwa akan menemukan kelegaannya di alam barzakh dan akhirat, sedangkan kelegaan di dunia ini yang diikuti dengan syahwat akan menemukan kesusahannya di alam barzakh dan akhirat. Hendaklah engkau memilih salah satu dari keduanya: yang lebih baik, lebih menyenangkan, dan lebih kekal. Engkau boleh menyusahkan tubuh demi melegakan jiwa, tetapi jangan menyusahkan jiwa demi melegakan tubuh; kelegaan dan kesusahan jiwa itu lebih besar dan lebih kekal, sedangkan kelegaan dan kesusahan tubuh itu lebih singkat dan lebih ringan.

Orang yang bijak tidak akan pernah memerintahkan manusia untuk meninggalkan kehidupan dunia; karena mereka tidak mampu meninggalkannya, tetapi ia akan memerintahkan mereka untuk meninggalkan dosa-dosa. Meninggalkan kehidupan dunia adalah keutamaan bagi mereka, sedangkan meninggalkan dosa adalah ibadah yang wajib. Bagaimana mungkin mereka diperintahkan melakukan keutamaan sementara mereka tidak

The العقل behind going astray is refusing the oath, accepting it unwillingly, accept it بينما lacking power and determination, or failing to urge themselves لفهم it, think about it, work وفقاً لـ it, or fulfill it. The oath was offered to them بينما still in their youth and observing religion as a habit and وفقاً لـ what they used to see their الوالدان doing. They received the oath بينما feeling satisfied with what they saw from their الوالدان and ancestors. This is not enough for whoever aims his intention and القلب at الفهم the oath and working وفقاً لـ it as إذا this oath applied to him alone and it was said to him: think about what this oath contains and work وفقاً لـ it. إذا he did not receive his oath in this الطريق, he would follow his ancestors, the habits of his الأسرة, friends, neighbors, and the people of his country. He would be satisfied to take religion as a habit. إذا الشيطان seduces him and reaches his intention and determination, it would accuse him of clanship and zealotry to his fathers and ancestors and convince him that this is الحق and whatever disagrees with it is false; الهداية would be considered الشر and الشر would be considered الهداية. This would indeed be fanaticism, which is not based on العلم. As he is satisfied للاتباع his الأسرة and nation, he will have what they have and will أيضاً have to pay what they are paying. He is far removed from الهداية and الله abandons him to whoever he took as a friend. Through this oath - he would know الله. الله will know that he knows Him, His ~ attributes, names, doings, and judgments. The servant will AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 279 know that الله is self-existing, enables others to exist, is in no need of anyone بينما all others are in need of Him. That He established Himself on the Throne above all His creatures, that He sees, hears, accepts, becomes angry, loves, hates, manages the affairs of His kingdom, orders, and forbids, sends His الرسل to every place in His kingdom bearing His words, which are heard by whoever wishes from among His creatures. He stands firm on العدل, awards وفقاً لـ both الخير and bad, is the Most Forbearing, the Most Merciful, the All-Thankful, the Most Generous, and the Well-doer. He is described with all perfection and is far above all defects, and He has no equal. The servant can see the
